title: Purchasing Assistant (QuickBooks Focused)
description:
Location: Remote (U.S. preferred)

Full-Time | Finance & Operations Support | Immediate Start



About the Role

We are seeking a highly detail-oriented Purchasing Assistant to support our construction and home improvement operations.

This role is responsible for accurately creating and managing purchase orders in QuickBooks, while also serving as a key communication link between vendors and project managers.

You will ensure materials are ordered correctly, tracked properly, and that internal teams are consistently updated on delivery timelines, delays, and order status.

Given the financial and operational impact of this role, accuracy, organization, and proactive communication are critical.



Key Responsibilities:

*Create and enter purchase orders in QuickBooks based on internal requests and project needs

*Verify pricing, quantities, and vendor details before submitting POs

*Maintain accurate records of vendors, materials, and purchase history

*Track order statuses and follow up with vendors on availability, lead times, and delivery schedules

*Proactively communicate with project managers regarding order status, including:

          - When materials are received

          - Expected delivery timelines

          - Delays, backorders, or issues

*Coordinate closely with project managers, sales, and operations to ensure alignment on project needs

*Reconcile purchase orders with invoices and flag any discrepancies

*Maintain organized purchasing and financial documentation

*Assist with cost tracking and basic reporting as needed

*Identify opportunities to improve purchasing workflows and communication processes



What We’re Looking For:

*Extremely detail-oriented — accuracy is non-negotiable

*Strong communicator — able to clearly and proactively update internal teams

*Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple orders and timelines

*Self-starter who takes ownership and follows through

*Comfortable working in a fast-paced, project-driven environment

*High level of accountability when handling financial and operational data



Required Experience & Skills:

*Hands-on QuickBooks experience (required) — especially purchase orders, vendors, and basic workflows

*Prior experience in purchasing, accounting, or operations support

*Strong understanding of purchase orders, vendor coordination, and order tracking

*Proficiency in Excel and/or Google Sheets

*Strong data entry skills with a focus on accuracy

*Ability to manage timelines and communicate updates effectively



Nice to Have (Not Required)

*Experience in construction, home improvement, or materials purchasing

*Familiarity with suppliers, lead times, and job-based purchasing

*Basic bookkeeping or accounting knowledge

*Experience with job costing or project-based workflows



Why This Role Matters:

*Direct impact on project timelines and financial accuracy

*Key role in keeping operations organized and projects moving

*Opportunity to grow into purchasing, operations, or accounting leadership

*Stable, long-term position within a growing company



For compensation, it will be $800 per month, and depending on experience, we would be willing to pay more for experienced candidates.

title: Vertical Video Editor (9:16) — DTC Hot Sauce Brand
description:
US-based DTC hot sauce brand (Creative Capsaicin LLC, Texas) hiring a full-time vertical video editor. Video editing only — no stills, no graphic design, no copywriting. Captions and stills are handled separately by our team.



THE ROLE

You receive raw footage plus a written daily brief (we already specify cut lengths, end-card text, hook line, and posting platform). Your job: cut fast, cut clean, hit deadlines. We don't need creative direction back from you — just strong execution.



DAILY VOLUME: 20–30 finished cuts per day, 7-day rotation

PRIMARY FORMAT: 9:16 vertical, MP4, 4K source ? 1080p delivery



CUT SPEC PER CONCEPT:

- A) 35s master (Reel + TikTok)

- B) 15s mid (Story + paid ad variant)

- C) 8s hook (paid ad hook test)

- Plus: 4:5 reframe of the B and C cuts for Meta Feed placements



So each concept is roughly 5 final files. The 9:16 master is the time-eater; reframes should template fast (5–10 min each with Auto Reframe / Smart Cropping).



REQUIRED TOOLS (must own):

- Premiere Pro OR DaVinci Resolve OR CapCut Pro

- Auto Reframe / Smart Cropping experience

- Dropbox for delivery

- Strong upload speeds (4K source files)



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE:

- 2+ years editing vertical short-form (Reels, TikTok, Shorts) for DTC or food/beverage brands

- Sample reel showing 9:16 product work — pour shots, two-person demos, ASMR food

- Reads English briefs cleanly — no creative back-and-forth needed

- Comfortable with same-day turnaround



HARD RULES (non-negotiable):

- On-camera talent is the Owner and the VP of Sales only — no other faces

- No discount codes burned into limited-edition drop creative

- Any text overlay must follow brand language: "Made in Texas" (never "Made in Boerne"), no "barrel-aged" or distillery references, no "Astros" (use "Daikin Park" if Houston baseball comes up), bottles are 8 oz

- Pour shots must keep the bottle label clean and forward-facing



SCHEDULE: 40 hrs/week, daily 9:00 PM Central Time delivery deadline. Work your own hours — Philippines overnight = our morning, which works perfectly.



PAY: $550–600/week, paid weekly via Wise, contractor basis. Rate within range based on test performance.



HIRING PROCESS:

1. Apply with reel + 1-paragraph note on why this role fits you

2. Paid $50 test cut — we send raw footage + a written brief, you deliver 3 cuts (35s/15s/8s in 9:16, plus a 4:5 reframe of the 15s) within 48 hours. Paid via Wise regardless of outcome.

3. If the test lands, start the following Monday at full rate.



REPLY WITH:

- Portfolio link

- Weekly availability (hours and timezone)

- Editing software you own (specifically: which Auto Reframe tool you use)

title: Virtual Assistant :: Research, Reporting & Platform Operations for Indie Music Marketing Agency
description:
Virtual Assistant :: Research, Reporting & Platform Operations for Indie Music Marketing Agency



Rate: $5-7/hr USD (based on experience)

Hours: 30 hrs/week to start, with clear path to full-time (40 hrs/week) within 60 days based on fit and workload

Pay cadence: Weekly for the first 2 months, then we'll discuss whether to continue weekly or move to bi-weekly

Trial: 1-week paid trial, then long-term



#About Us



We're Barely (barely.nyc / barely.ai), an indie music marketing agency based in Brooklyn, NYC. We run marketing campaigns for independent artists and record labels, and we're building a marketing platform (barely.ai) for the indie music world.



This is our second VA hire. The first runs our Stan account ops. This role is different :: it's research-heavy, data-focused, and supports both the agency operations and the platform side of the business. Real room to grow with us as we scale.



# The Core Job



Six main areas of work. Roughly equal split, with daily/weekly/monthly cadence varying.



1. Lead research & list-building



We're growing on the agency side and need a steady pipeline of qualified leads. You'll help us build and maintain lists of:

- Independent record labels we should be reaching out to (size, genre fit, recent activity, contact info)

- Music-focused influencers and creators for fractional media campaigns

- Artists who fit our service tiers



We'll give you clear criteria for what makes a "qualified" lead. Your job is to find them, gather the relevant info into our spreadsheet/Notion templates, and keep the lists fresh.



2. Client audit data gathering



When we onboard a new label or artist client, we run a digital health audit. A lot of the audit work is pulling data from the same sources every time:

- Spotify for Artists (we have access to all our clients' accounts)

- Public Spotify pages

- Public Instagram, TikTok, YouTube

- Filling in standardized scorecard templates



You pull the data into our templates so we (the agency leads) can focus on the strategic interpretation.



3. Daily campaign data capture



We run Spotify playlist growth campaigns for clients. Right now we capture about 15 minutes of data per day :: stream counts, save rates, ad spend, key metrics across multiple campaigns. We want to capture more data than we currently do, and we want it captured every day - including weekends.



This is short, but consistent. About 20-30 minutes per day, 7 days a week. You own the daily snapshot and flag anything that looks unusual.



4. Twice-monthly client report prep



We send monthly reports to coaching clients alongside their invoices. Reports go out on either the 1st or the 15th of each month, depending on the client's billing cycle - so there are two crunch windows each month, not one.



Each report pulls the same shape of data :: streaming metrics, social growth, ad performance, content performance. The data assembly is rote (your part). The strategic interpretation is ours.



You'll own pulling the numbers, populating the report templates, generating the matching invoices in our platform, and flagging anything noteworthy in the data. The heavy lift clusters around the last few days of each month and around the 12th-14th.



5. Platform operations (barely.ai)



We're building barely.ai, our marketing platform for indie artists. As an EA on the team, you'll join client workspaces in the platform and take care of recurring operational work:

- Adding tracking pixels during artist onboarding

- Creating landing pages and smart link pages

- Generating invoices through the platform

- Other point-and-click operational tasks inside the app



This is "power user" work :: structured, repeatable, but inside our software. We'll train you on the platform during your first weeks.



**Bonus value :: dogfooding.** Because you'll use the platform daily, you'll naturally find bugs, awkward UI, missing features, and friction points. Part of your job is to clearly write up these issues so we can fix them. You don't need to know how to code - just describe what you tried, what happened, and what you expected. We'll handle the rest.



6. Competitive intel & best-practice synthesis



We follow a number of music industry podcasts, marketing newsletters, and social accounts that publish actionable best practices. We're behind on a lot of it. We want someone who can:

- Listen to / read assigned podcasts and newsletters weekly

- Pull out concrete, actionable tactics ("post a story about merch every Wednesday")

- Translate them into a short weekly digest we can apply to client campaigns

- Track what other indie marketing shops are doing in our space



This is the kind of work that compounds in value over months. We need someone who can do it consistently and develop pattern recognition over time.



#What We're Looking For



- Reliable and consistent - especially on the daily data capture and twice-monthly reporting cycles. If you can't commit to weekends and to crunch periods around the 1st and the 15th of each month, this isn't the role.

- Strong written English - you'll be working in our spreadsheets, Notion docs, weekly digests, and platform bug reports. Your writing needs to be clean enough that we can review and trust it without rewriting.

- Detail-oriented - audit data, lead research, monthly reports, and platform ops all punish carelessness. We'd rather you go slightly slower and get it right than rush and make us double-check.

- Comfortable with judgment calls - this isn't a pure script-follower role. You'll need to make small decisions ("is this lead actually a fit?" "is this number weird?" "is this a bug or just confusing UI?") and surface them to us.

- Spreadsheet comfort - Google Sheets specifically.

- Notion - we run our whole agency in Notion. Prior experience is a plus.



#Nice-to-Haves



Not required but call them out if you have them:



- Familiarity with indie/alternative music - massive force multiplier across lead research, audit interpretation, and competitive intel

- Spotify for Artists or general music industry exposure - helps you parse what you're looking at

- Basic understanding of Meta ads / digital marketing - so the campaign data isn't a black box

- Comfort with web apps and SaaS tools - faster ramp on the platform ops work, and helpful for writing clear bug reports. We can train, but it's nice to have.

- Light Canva skills - for occasional internal docs, lead enrichment graphics, or formatting the weekly digest. Not video/social content creation - that's our other VA's role.

- Any experience with B2B lead gen, sales support, market research, or VA work for marketing teams - this work is closer to that than to pure admin



#Schedule



- Weekday hours flexible, but *daily campaign data capture needs to happen 7 days a week*, including a quick weekend check-in (it's short - 10-20 min)

- Reporting + invoicing crunch periods :: last few days of the month and the 12th-14th of each month - flexibility to push hours up those windows is helpful

- We're in NYC (Eastern Time). You don't need to be online during US hours but some overlap is helpful for questions.



#Trial & Long-Term



We start with a 1-week paid trial on real work :: a small lead research task, an audit data pull on one of our existing clients, and a few days of the daily campaign capture. If it goes well, we move to long-term.



The first month is 30 hours/week so we can both calibrate. After 30-60 days, if you're crushing it and the workload is there (which we expect it will be), we ramp to 40 hours/week.



*On pay cadence :: we pay weekly for the first 2 months.* No prepayment or retainer required from you - we just want to make sure you're getting paid frequently while we both build trust. After 2 months we'll discuss whether to continue weekly or move to bi-weekly.



We're not looking for a temporary gig. We want someone who can grow with us over years.



#How to Apply



Reply with:



1. Your name and a brief intro

2. Any relevant experience with research, data entry, lead gen, recurring reporting work, or VA work for marketing/sales teams

3. Your familiarity with music (any genres you love? any indie artists you follow?)

4. Your weekly availability and timezone

5. Whether weekend daily check-ins and the 1st/15th reporting crunches are something you can commit to long-term

6. The pay you are looking for



*At the top of your reply, write the word "barely" so we know you read this whole post.*



We'll respond to applications that follow these instructions. Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Phone Sales Rep - $100 Per Sale + Recurring Commission - Work From Home
description:
READ THE ENTIRE POST AND APPLY AT THE LINKS BELOW TO BE CONSIDERED



Advanced Marketing is a US-based digital marketing agency looking for experienced phone sales reps to sell our $500 custom website offer to small business owners in the United States. You will work from home, use our dialer and CRM, and follow our proven sales script to close deals.



This is a commission-only role with fast payouts and long-term recurring income. Every website you sell pays you $100 USD immediately. If the client later signs up for our monthly marketing services, you earn an additional 20% recurring commission every month they remain a client.



Top performers earn $1,500 to $2,000+ USD per month within 90 days.



---



WHAT YOU WILL DO



- Make 150 to 200 outbound phone calls per day to US small businesses

- Follow our proven script to pitch our $500 website offer

- Close sales and fill out a simple onboarding form for the client

- Confirm the client received their welcome
 from our team

- Hit daily call volume and weekly sales goals

- Attend team training calls and sales coaching sessions



You will NOT build websites, handle customer support, or manage billing. Your only job is to dial, pitch, and close.



---



COMPENSATION



- $100 USD per website sold (paid weekly via direct bank transfer)

- 20% recurring commission on any marketing retainer the client signs later

- Marketing retainers average $1,000 to $5,000 USD per month

- Recurring commissions paid every month the client stays active, for as long as you are employed with us

- No cap on earnings



EXAMPLE: If you close 3 websites per day, that is $300 USD per day or approximately $6,000 USD per month in base commission alone. Plus recurring bonuses on top.



---



WHAT WE PROVIDE



- Proven sales script and pitch

- Pre-qualified US small business leads

- GoHighLevel CRM and dialer access

- Complete training via Zoom

- Ongoing coaching and team support

- Weekly payouts via your preferred method



---



REQUIREMENTS



- Excellent spoken English with neutral or American accent

- Minimum 1 year of phone sales, cold calling, BPO, or appointment setting experience

- Reliable high-speed internet (at least 25 Mbps)

- Quiet home workspace with no background noise

- Computer or laptop capable of running a web-based dialer and CRM

- Quality USB headset with noise-cancelling microphone

- Available to work US business hours (typically 9 PM to 5 AM Philippine time)

- Able to attend a training Zoom call (6 AM Eastern US time / 6 PM Philippine time)

- Willing to complete a 1-hour sales roleplay assessment after training

- Stable electricity and backup power solution preferred



---



PREFERRED EXPERIENCE



- Previous experience selling

description:
Real World Technologies is looking for a motivated and results-driven Sales Representative to help grow our VoIP phone service business. This position is ideal for someone who is confident on the phone, comfortable with cold calling, and skilled at turning prospects into paying customers.

Use our platform to prepare and deliver quotes

title: Shopify CRO & Landing Page Specialist (Remote)
description:

About the Client:

We are a high-performance marketing agency based in Munich, Germany, currently building our CRO department from scratch. We work with a diverse range of eCommerce brands—from smaller shops needing foundational trust and AOV improvements to large-scale brands doing €10M+/year where every data-driven change has a massive revenue impact.



About the Role:

We are looking for a Shopify CRO & Landing Page Optimization Specialist who thinks in revenue, not just aesthetics. We value impact over job titles; whether you are a highly-motivated Junior or a seasoned Senior, we care about your ability to explain strategy, execute tests, and move the needle on CVR and AOV.



This role is unique: You will manage client eCommerce stores, but you may also work internally on our B2B funnels (Lead magnets, VSLs, and booking pages). We want a "solution-bringer" who helps us define the tech stack and processes for this new department.



You will be responsible for:

- eCommerce CRO: Optimizing PDPs, listicles, advertorials, and checkout flows to increase Revenue per Visitor.

- B2B Optimization: Improving internal funnels for lead opt-ins and booked meetings.

- Strategic Communication: Recording clear Loom audits to explain the "why" behind your optimizations.

- Data-Driven Testing: Moving from hypothesis to test to iteration.



Must-Have Skills & Experience:

- Strong Shopify Expertise: Deep hands-on experience with the platform.

- Conversion Focus: Proven results improving CVR, AOV, or Funnel metrics.

- Psychology & Design: Understanding of buying psychology and modern Western design standards.

- Commercial Mindset: Ability to prioritize tasks based on revenue impact, not just "looking pretty."

- Tech Versatility: Experience with page builders, bundling apps, and heatmapping tools (you tell us what’s best!).



How to Apply, please complete this form:





Note: This role is NOT for "task-takers" or "app-installers." We are looking for a strategic partner to help us build a department. If you think in testing frameworks and funnel leverage, we want to meet you.

title: ASMR Raw Egg Peeling Creator & YouTube Live Streamer
description:
We are looking for someone to create relaxing ASMR-style content focused on slowly peeling raw eggs, and to live stream the process on YouTube.



The goal is to carefully remove the outer shell while keeping the inner membrane intact. The content is slow, relaxing, and visually satisfying. No talking or on-camera presence is required.



Reference Channel: 



What you will do:



Live stream the egg peeling process on YouTube (calm, relaxing streams).



Carefully peel raw eggs while keeping the inner membrane intact.



Move slowly and maintain a relaxing, steady rhythm.



Ensure good lighting, framing, and high-quality audio (natural ASMR sounds only).



Follow a consistent visual style similar to the reference channel.



What you need:



iPhone 11 or newer (or an equivalent smartphone with a very good camera).



A highly stable internet connection suitable for seamless live streaming.



Ring light or soft, warm lighting setup.



Raw eggs (you will need to purchase these locally).



A small comedone extractor or similar precision tool to start peeling.



A completely quiet filming environment to capture pure ASMR audio.



Requirements:



Extreme patience and very steady hands.



Ability to perform slow, repetitive actions without rushing.



Ability to manage a basic YouTube live streaming setup.



Comfortable streaming for extended periods of time.



To apply, please send:



The exact phone model you will use for filming.



Confirmation that your internet can handle live streaming on YouTube.



A short sample video showing steady, close-up hand work (it doesn't have to be an egg, just something showing your camera quality, lighting, and steady hands).

title: Instructional Designer – Digital Learning & Course Development
description:
Overview

Vucko Ventures is the parent company of a group of businesses based in Laval, Quebec, including a2z strategic consulting, Fleuriste La Mode, and Fleurir La Mobile.

Within this group, a2z strategic consulting is expanding its digital learning portfolio across 11 course categories, with over 200 courses designed for workplace learning, leadership development, and professional growth.



We are looking for an Instructional Designer – Digital Learning & Course Development who will take ownership of enhancing, structuring, and scaling our course content into high-quality, engaging, and market-ready learning products.



You will not be starting from scratch. You will be working with existing content and transforming it into modern, structured, and highly engaging learning experiences.



This is a remote, full-time role, five days per week, with flexibility required based on production timelines and priorities.



What You Will Own



Course Enhancement & Development

• Review and enhance existing course content across 11 categories

• Modernize content to align with current workplace realities and learning expectations

• Ensure consistency in tone, structure, and instructional quality across all courses



Microlearning & Course Structuring

• Break down larger courses into microcourses and modular learning units

• Structure content for flexibility, scalability, and learner engagement

• Apply practical instructional design models to support real-world application



Learning Asset Development

• Develop all course peripherals, including:

• PowerPoint presentations

• Participant guides

• Instructor manuals

• Course outlines

• Activities and case studies

• Spot-checks and knowledge checks



Digital & Video Content Creation

• Prepare content for YouTube and video-based learning

• Work with existing avatars and tools to support video production

• Translate course material into short, engaging video formats



Branding & Standardization

• Ensure all courses align with a2z’s branding, structure, and learning approach

• Apply consistent templates, frameworks, and visual standards

• Maintain quality and cohesion across the full course catalogue



Certification & Recognition Assets

• Prepare course certificates and completion materials

• Develop badge structures for learners

• Ensure alignment with professional learning expectations



Marketing Asset Support

• Support the creation of course-related marketing assets, including:

o Course descriptions

o Advertorial content

o Promotional blurbs



• Ensure alignment between learning content and marketing messaging



What Success Looks Like

• Courses are clear, engaging, and consistent across categories

• Content is structured into scalable microlearning formats

• Course materials are complete, professional, and ready for delivery

• Video content is aligned, practical, and engaging

• Production timelines are met consistently

• The course catalogue becomes easier to sell, deliver, and scale





Key Performance Indicators

• Number of courses enhanced and completed

• Quality and consistency of instructional design

• Completion of full course asset packages

• Efficiency of content production

• Alignment with brand and learning standards

• Readiness of courses for delivery and sale





Who You Are

• You think in structure, clarity, and learner experience

• You enjoy taking existing content and making it better

• You are highly organized and detail-oriented

• You can move quickly without sacrificing quality

• You are comfortable working independently and managing multiple projects

• You take pride in producing polished, professional work



Experience

• 3+ years in instructional design, curriculum development, or learning design

• Experience developing workplace or professional learning content

• Experience creating course materials such as PPTs, guides, and activities

• Familiarity with eLearning tools (Articulate, LMS platforms, or similar) is an asset

• Experience with video-based learning or digital content creation is an asset



Work Structure

• Full-time, remote role

• Five days per week

• Flexible hours based on project timelines and deliverables



Why This Role

This is an opportunity to work with a large and growing course library and help transform it into a scalable, high-quality digital learning ecosystem.

You will play a direct role in shaping how our content is experienced, delivered, and monetized.

title: PPC Specialist
description:
KickCharge is seeking a full-time, experienced Pay Per Click Specialist to join a dynamic paid search team.



As a KickCharge PPC Specialist, you will drive traffic and ROI via paid client acquisition tactics for our clients. The candidate will regularly optimize paid search efforts to help achieve department and company goals. This position will be responsible for keyword research planning, campaign/bid management, copywriting, landing page optimization, and ROI tracking plus analysis.



Responsibilities Include:

Execute and improve all paid search tactics through hands-on day to day optimizations across paid channels (Google and Bing)

Strategize with PPC team

Perform ongoing keyword expansion including identification and implementation of keyword opportunities

Increase CTR with compelling ad copy enhancements and creation

Optimize landing pages for maximum conversions and implement re-marketing strategies

Management of ad expenditures with daily reporting, performance analysis, and bid optimization techniques

Collect and analyze data to identify trends and insights to achieve maximum ROI in paid search campaigns

Keep up-to-date with PPC best practices, keyword research tools, and industry plus competitor research

Providing detailed reports of progress



Requirements:

Bachelor's degree in Advertising, Marketing, Communications, Business, or related fields

Google Analytics and Google Adwords Certified

Must have at least two years of experience in digital marketing and PPC.

Excellent understanding of contemporary digital marketing principles, strategies, and best practices

Synergized understanding of other forms of digital marketing, including content marketing, SEO, conversion optimization, landing page optimization, paid marketing, re-targeting, etc.

Proven experience managing PPC campaigns across Google and Bing.

Solid understanding of performance marketing, conversion, and online customer acquisition

In-depth experience with website analytics tools Including Google Analytics, Optmyzer, etc.

Strong english & communication skills a must.



About Us:

What does your day look like?

Closely monitoring campaign metrics to ensure the best performance. CPAs, CTR, Impression Shares, Conversions.

Analyzing campaigns to see how they are performing, and make adjustments based on data to improve results.

Auditing accounts in search of potential improvements.

Communicating with teaUpgrade to see actual infombers to ensure results and expectations are being met.

Crafting unique and engaging ad copy.

Effective keyword research and planning.

Overall campaign adjustments and optimization.

This position requires the ability to multitask, work with a team, and above all, to drive results for our clients.



Responsibilities Include:

•Regularly monitor organic SERP traction by using tools like Google Analytics, Ahrefs, SEMRush and BrightLocal

•Regularly monitor competitor link-building strategies

•Regularly monitor Google Business Profile rankings

•Suggest on-page edits to improve SEO strength

•Support our SEO Citation Building Team

•Support our Web team when preparing website migrations

•Suggest optimization of web pages for maximum ranking and implement 

•Collect and analyze data to identify trends and insights to achieve maximum ROI in organic search campaigns

•Keep up-to-date with SEO best practices, keyword research tools, and industry-plus competitor research



Requirements:

•Bachelor's degree in Advertising, Marketing, Communications, Business, or related fields

•Google Analytics Certified A Plus

•In-depth experience with Ahrefs

•Must be able to speak English fluently in written and verbal form.

•Must have at least two years of experience in digital marketing and SEO.

•Excellent understanding of contemporary digital marketing principles, strategies, and best practices

•Synergized understanding of other forms of digital marketing, including content marketing, PPC, conversion optimization, landing page optimization, paid marketing, re-targeting, etc.

•Proven experience managing SEO campaigns across Google and Bing.

•Technical SEO skills a plus. 

•Google Business Profile experience a plus.

•Solid understanding of performance marketing, conversion, and online customer acquisition



About Us:

What does your day look like?

•Closely monitoring campaign metrics to ensure the best performance.

•Analyzing SEO strategies to see how they are performing, and make adjustments based on data to improve results.

•Auditing accounts in search of potential improvements.

•Communicating with tea
mbers to ensure results and expectations are being met.

•Effective backlink research and planning.

•Overall campaign adjustments and optimization.



To Apply:

Please send a cover letter and CV/Résumé outlining your experience and certifications, along with a video of YOU explaining your SEO philosophy, and why you'd made a great fit to the team.

title: Bookkeeper/Financial Analyst - Multi-Entity Operations
description:
Location: Remote

Hours: Full-time, with 2-4 hours overlap with US CST





About Us



We operate a portfolio of technology businesses including an IT consulting firm serving medical/dental practices, a gaming studio, and other ventures. We're transitioning to ERPNext for our accounting operations and need a detail-oriented financial professional to own our bookkeeping and reporting functions across multiple entities.





Core Responsibilities



Daily/Weekly Operations



Reconcile bank statements across multiple business entities

Categorize and track all expenses with detailed spend analysis

Process and record transactions in ERPNext

Maintain clean separation between multiple LLCs and business units

Perform month-end close procedures



Monthly Invoicing & Collections



Generate and send all client invoices on the 1st of every month

Track invoice aging and follow up on overdue accounts

Manage accounts receivable and collection efforts

Coordinate with the operations team on billable services/hours

Maintain customer billing information and terms



Inventory Management



Maintain inventory levels in ERPNext

Monitor inventory costs and usage patterns

Coordinate with the team on reorder points

Track inventory across multiple locations/projects



Financial Reporting



Generate monthly P&L statements for each entity

Create cost center analysis (e.g., "What is the company costing us monthly?")

Develop profitability reports by business line

Prepare management reports showing cash flow and burn rates

Track project-level profitability for client engagements



Tax Preparation Support



Prepare quarterly federal tax estimate calculations

Prepare state tax estimate calculations (Nebraska primarily)

Organize documentation for CPA review and filing

Maintain tax calendar and ensure deadlines are tracked



Business Intelligence



Identify spending patterns and cost-saving opportunities

Track KPIs across business units

Answer ad-hoc questions like "How much are we spending on software subscriptions?"

Create financial dashboards in ERPNext



Required Qualifications



Must-Haves



3+ years bookkeeping experience with US companies

Experience with multi-entity accounting

Strong understanding of US GAAP

Experience with recurring monthly billing/invoicing

Proficiency in bank reconciliation and financial statement preparation

Experience preparing documentation for US quarterly taxes

Advanced Excel/spreadsheet skills

Excellent written English communication

Available for calls during US business hours as needed



Strongly Preferred



ERPNext experience (or similar ERP systems like Odoo, NetSuite, SAP)

Experience with technology/IT services companies

Inventory management experience

Cost accounting or management accounting background

Experience with project-based profitability tracking

Familiarity with US payroll systems (we use ADP)

Experience with gaming or software development accounting



Technical Requirements



Stable internet connection (must be reliable for 1st of month invoicing)

Comfortable learning new software quickly

Experience with cloud-based accounting tools

Ability to work independently with minimal supervision

Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills



What Success Looks Like



All invoices sent by noon CST on the 1st of every month (no exceptions)

Month-end books closed within 3 business days

Quarterly tax packages ready 2 weeks before deadlines

AR aging kept under 30 days average

Inventory counts are accurate and updated weekly

Management has real-time visibility into financial performance

Clean audit trail for all transactions

Proactive identification of financial insights, not just reporting



Growth Opportunities





This role has significant growth potential. As we scale from 16 to 30+ employees, this position could evolve into a Financial Operations Manager role with broader responsibilities and team management opportunities.





To Apply



Upload your resume highlighting US client experience.



Message us:

Your resume highlighting US client experience.

A brief note explaining your experience with multi-entity accounting

Your experience with monthly billing cycles and inventory management

Your monthly salary expectation in USD

Your time zone and hours of availability

Important: In your application, describe a time when you identified a significant cost-saving opportunity through financial analysis



Applications without US company experience or the cost-saving example will not be considered.





Note: We value honesty and continuous learning. If you don't know something, we expect you to say so and learn it, rather than pretend expertise you don't have. We invest heavily in training the right people.

s, mailing addresses)

title: Executive Assistant to Founder
description:
Executive Assistant to Founder — AI & Ecommerce Company



I’m the founder of a growing AI consultancy and training business in the ecommerce space. I work with agencies, brands, and teams across the US, UK, and Europe — helping them integrate AI into their operations. The business is growing fast, and I need someone who can grow with it.



This is not a typical VA role. I’m not looking for someone to answer
s and manage a calendar (though you’ll do some of that too). I’m looking for a sharp, resourceful person who can figure things out independently — someone who sees a new tool, a new platform, or a new challenge and thinks “let me work this out” rather than “no one taught me this.”



What you’ll actually do



The tasks will change. That’s the point. On any given week, you might:



• Research a new platform or tool I’ve never used, learn it, and brief me on how it works

• Format and send a client presentation or proposal

• Set up and manage a cold
 outreach campaign

• Build a simple landing page or website section on a platform like Carrd, Webflow, or WordPress

• Manage and moderate a WhatsApp or Slack community

• Analyse data in spreadsheets and pull out the key insights

• Research competitors, market trends, or potential partners

• Post content on social media and track what’s working

• Help coordinate training sessions, webinars, or events

• Handle inbox triage and scheduling across time zones



The common thread: every task requires you to think, not just execute. If I have to explain every detail and check every step, this isn’t the right fit. 



What matters more than your CV



• You’re a natural self-learner. You figure things out using Google, YouTube, AI tools, forums — whatever it takes. You don’t wait to be trained.

• You take initiative. When you see something that could be done better, you flag it or just fix it.

• You communicate clearly. If you’re stuck, you say so early — with context on what you’ve already tried.

• You’re reliable and organised. You track your own tasks, meet deadlines, and follow up without being chased.

• You’re comfortable with ambiguity. I’ll often give you the “what” and the “why” but not the “how” — that’s your job to figure out.

In the Google Form, write "Oranges" in the field "Anything Else To Add".



The details

• Hours: Full-time, 35–40 hours/week

• Location: Remote 

• Compensation: $500–$1,000/month depending on experience, with room to grow as the business grows

• Communication: Daily standups and regular check-ins. We work closely together — this is a partnership, not a set-and-forget arrangement.

• Language: Fluent English is essential. All communication, content, and client work is in English.

• Tools: Google Workspace, Slack, and a willingness to learn anything else. Experience with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) is a plus but not required.



Why this could be a great opportunity

This business is at an inflection point. I’m building something that didn’t exist two years ago, in one of the fastest-moving industries in the world. The right person won’t just be an assistant — they’ll become essential to how this business runs. That means real responsibility, real growth, and compensation that reflects your impact over time.

How to apply:



Do NOT apply through OnlineJobs.ph messaging. Instead, fill out this short application form:





Important: Read the entire job description and application form carefully before you start filling it in.

title: Full Time Dispatcher
description:
About the Role



We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Dispatcher with a positive attitude and excellent communication skills. The ideal candidate will have expertise in load negotiation, scheduling, and route planning, while adhering to DOT and ELD regulations.



Vehicles Being Dispatched



26-Foot Box Trucks (10,000 lb weight capacity) – Dock-high with lift gate

Sprinter Vans / Cargo Vans / Ram ProMaster (High Roof)



About Us



Eveready Express is a well-established leader in the trucking, courier, logistics, and distribution industries, founded in 1984. We pride ourselves on honesty, integrity, and mutual respect.



Key Responsibilities

Dispatching Vehicles



Negotiate rates with drivers while aiming for a minimum 40% margin, and assign vehicles to specific routes and deliveries based on customer orders, schedules, and driver availability.



Communication with Drivers



Maintain constant communication with drivers by providing instructions, updates, and support throughout their routes.



Routing and Scheduling



Plan efficient routes to minimize travel time, fuel costs, and vehicle wear. Adjust schedules as needed to accommodate changes or unexpected events.



Tracking Shipments



Monitor shipments in real time using tracking systems and GPS technology. Address delays or issues as they arise.



Customer Interaction



Serve as the primary point of contact for customers after dispatch, providing delivery updates, addressing inquiries, and resolving concerns or complaints promptly.



Documentation



Maintain accurate records of trucking activities, including driver logs, delivery schedules, and customer communications. Ensure compliance with company policies and regulations (e.g., BOL, POD).



Problem-Solving



Resolve logistical problems or emergencies such as vehicle breakdowns, traffic delays, or adverse weather conditions.



Safety Compliance



Ensure trucks and drivers comply with safety regulations and company policies. Regularly verify driver credentials, vehicle maintenance, and adherence to transportation laws in collaboration with HR.



Team Collaboration



Work closely with Customer Service, Fleet Drivers, Billing, Sales, HR, and Warehouse staff to coordinate operations and improve efficiency.



Continuous Improvement



Identify and suggest improvements to dispatching processes, customer satisfaction, and operational efficiency. Offer feedback and innovative solutions to the management team.



Working on Load Boards



Bid on Sylectus, DAT, Amazon Relay, and other broker platforms during the off-peak season.



Qualifications



• At least 2 years of experience in the U.S. logistics industry, with proven experience in dispatching, logistics coordination, or transportation management.



• Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to effectively interact with drivers, customers, and tea
mbers.



• Excellent organizational and multitasking skills, with strong attention to detail and the ability to prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment.



• Proficiency in dispatching software, GPS systems, and other relevant technology tools.



• Knowledge of transportation regulations, safety procedures, and industry best practices.



• Ability to remain calm under pressure, with a problem-solving mindset and flexibility to adapt to changing circumstances.



• Availability to work irregular hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, to ensure 24/7 coverage.



Hardware & Technical Requirements



• Processor: 2.0 GHz or higher (Intel Core i5/i7 preferred)

• RAM: Minimum 8 GB

• Disk Space: 100 GB of free space

• A second monitor for multitasking

• Backup computer

• Noise-canceling headset

• Internet: Minimum 10 Mbps wired connection for both primary and backup ISPs

• USB sticks are not allowed for backup ISP connections



Perks of the Job



• Competitive weekly salary

• Opportunity to receive HMO benefits

• Opportunities for advancement within the company



To Apply



Please send an
 with the subject line:



“I want to dispatch for Eveready”



to 



Your
 must include:



• A voice recording (via Speakpipe or Vocaroo) explaining why you are a good fit for the role

• A PDF copy of your resume

• A photo of your workstation

• A screenshot of your internet speed test from speedtest.net



Please follow all instructions above. Only applicants who demonstrate genuine interest in the role will be considered.



If selected, you will be invited to a brief
 or Google Meet interview, which will require a video call.



Equal Opportunity Employer



Eveready Express is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, color, disability, medical condition, genetic information, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, marital status, gender identity or expression, sex (including pregnancy), age, veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.



We are committed to fostering a culture of diversity, equity, and inclusion.



Note



Eveready Express reserves the right to modify job duties and assignments at any time. The job description provided is not all-encompassing and may change depending on business needs.

title: Google Ads Specialist — Local Lead Generation (Search Campaigns)
description:
Google Ads Specialist — Local Lead Generation (Search Campaigns)

Remote | Part-Time or Full-Time

Work Hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST



Please Read Before Applying

This is not a role for someone who boosts posts or runs Display campaigns and calls it Google Ads experience.

We are looking for a Google Ads Search specialist who understands:



How to build Search campaigns from scratch for local service businesses

Conversion tracking — and why you never launch without it

The difference between a cheap click and a qualified lead

Why Google's default settings are designed to waste your client's money



If you've built, launched, optimised, and reported on Google Ads Search campaigns for trades, health, legal, real estate, or other local service businesses — keep reading.



What You'll Do

You will own Google Ads end to end — from campaign build to weekly optimisation to monthly reporting — across multiple local service clients in Australia.

This is a fast-paced, systemised environment. We have documented SOPs for every phase of Google Ads delivery. You are expected to follow them precisely while also thinking critically about what each client needs. Speed matters — campaigns need to be built correctly and launched on tight timelines without compromising quality.



Build Search campaigns from scratch following our 8-phase Campaign Build SOP — client brief, keyword research, campaign structure, conversion tracking, ad copy, landing page verification, campaign settings, pre-launch review

Complete our 118-point Pre-Launch Checklist before every campaign goes live — no exceptions

Install and verify conversion tracking via Google Tag Manager before any campaign launches — phone calls, form submissions, and bookings

Write high-converting RSA ad copy for local service businesses — minimum 3 RSAs per ad group, 10+ headlines each, ad strength Good or Excellent

Set up and manage all ad extensions — call, sitelinks, callouts, structured snippets, images, location

Configure campaign settings correctly — Presence-only location targeting, Display Network off, Search Partners off, auto-apply recommendations disabled, Manual CPC for new accounts

Run structured weekly optimisation sessions — search terms, negative keywords, keyword bids, RSA asset performance, budget pacing, impression share, landing page CVR

Run monthly reviews — Quality Score deep dive, audience insights, extension performance, bidding strategy review

Run quarterly account audits — full structure review, keyword strategy, competitive landscape, tracking verification

Work with our 20 automated monitoring scripts that run daily — review the morning digest, action all critical and warning alerts same day

Build and verify landing pages in GoHighLevel (or verify existing landing pages) — message match, mobile speed, form testing, conversion tag installation

Report clearly on performance — CPA vs target, lead volume, what changed, what is planned next





Must-Have Experience (Non-Negotiable)



Proven experience building and managing Google Ads Search campaigns for local service businesses (trades, health, fitness, legal, real estate, automotive, or similar)

Strong understanding of conversion tracking — you can install tags via GTM, test them, verify them in Google Ads, and troubleshoot when tracking breaks

Experience with keyword research and intent-based campaign structure — you know the difference between transactional, commercial, and informational intent and you build ad groups around tight keyword themes

Experience writing RSA ad copy that converts — not just high CTR, but qualified leads

Understanding of bidding strategy progression — Manual CPC for new accounts, when to move to Maximise Conversions, when Target CPA is appropriate, and why you never switch with less than 30 conversions per month

Strong negative keyword management — you proactively review search terms and maintain negative keyword lists

Ability to analyse performance beyond CPC — you think in terms of CPA, CVR, lead quality, impression share, and Quality Score

Experience managing multiple client accounts simultaneously

Ability to follow structured SOPs and checklists while still applying judgment — you work within a system, not around it

Ability to work fast under pressure without cutting corners — campaigns need to be built right and launched on time



If your Google Ads experience is only Display, Shopping, or Performance Max — this is not the right fit.

If you have only managed one account or only worked on large national brands — this is not the right fit.



Nice to Have (Optional)



Experience with Google Ads Scripts — we run 20 automated monitoring scripts across all client accounts and you may be involved in maintaining or configuring them

Experience with GoHighLevel (GHL) — landing pages, funnels, form setup, CRM pipeline connection

Experience with Google Tag Manager beyond basic tag installation — event tracking, custom triggers, debugging

Understanding of Google Analytics 4 — conversion events, organic vs paid attribution, audience creation

Experience building landing pages for local lead gen — message match, mobile-first design, form optimisation, speed testing

Familiarity with n8n or automation workflows — our monitoring scripts post to n8n which routes alerts to the team

Experience with Australian local service businesses specifically





Why Work With Us



Work directly with the founder — no layers of middle management

No micromanagement — but clear accountability through structured SOPs and daily automated reporting

Heavily systemised environment — you are not guessing what to do. Every phase of campaign build, optimisation, and reporting is documented

20 automated monitoring scripts catch issues before they cost money — you focus on strategy and optimisation, not manually checking if ads are still running

Long-term clients across multiple local service industries — stable, varied workload

You will have access to a design team and landing page builders — you focus on campaign performance

Remote role with consistent hours and weekly pay





Hours & Pay



Remote role (Philippines preferred)

Working hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST

Weekly pay via Wise

Part-time or full-time (based on performance and workload)

Long-term opportunity for the right operator





How to Apply (Important)

To be considered, please send:



A short intro or Loom video explaining:



Your Google Ads Search campaign experience

The industries you have worked with (especially local service businesses)

How you structure a new Search campaign from scratch — walk us through your process

How you approach weekly optimisation — what do you check and in what order





2–3 examples of Google Ads Search campaigns you've managed — screenshots showing campaign structure, results, or before/after performance improvements

Your timezone and daily availability



Applications without specific Google Ads Search campaign experience will not be reviewed. Do not apply if your experience is only Display or Shopping campaigns.



Final Filter

This role is for operators who build campaigns that generate leads — not impressions.

If you:

Check search terms before adjusting bids

Think in CPA and lead quality, not clicks and CTR

Can follow a system while still thinking critically



You'll thrive here.

title: AI-Fluent Operations Associate (Recruiting + Sales Support)
description:
AI-Fluent Operations Associate — Executive Search + AI Consulting (Remote, Full-Time)



About us

Two sister companies, one founder, one operator we need to hire.

The first is a boutique executive search firm placing engineers, quants, and operators at top hedge funds, fintechs, and fast-growing tech companies. Pinnacle Society firm, 1,000+ placements, 20+ years of track record.

The second is an AI solutions agency. We help companies design, build, and deploy AI workflows and agents that actually move the business, not demoware.



The founder runs lean and runs hot. Heavy automation, multiple active searches, real consulting engagements, daily LinkedIn content. You'd be the first key hire across both.



The role

You'll be the human in the loop on an AI-driven operation that spans two businesses. We already have the systems: a custom AI agent stack, a CRM, web search and enrichment tools, Gmail and LinkedIn automation, a content engine, and a consulting pipeline. What we need is someone who can drive these tools every day, keep both pipelines clean, and make every piece of output look like a human who actually cares wrote it.



This is not a traditional VA role. If you light up when you hear "MCP server," "Claude project," or "agent workflow," keep reading.



What you'll own (split roughly 60% recruiting / 40% AI consulting)



Recruiting side

Run daily sourcing through our AI agent stack. Clean and enrich candidates in the CRM. Build target lists from GitHub, conference speakers, and public web footprints. Draft LinkedIn InMails and Gmail outreach in the founder's voice (no dashes, "Hi [Name]," punchy subject lines, comp anchors, full contact block). Own Calendly scheduling, candidate prep notes, interview debriefs, references, and hiring manager updates.



AI consulting side

Sales pipeline support. Research target accounts (mid-market companies likely to need AI workflow help), find decision-makers, draft cold outreach sequences for founder review, manage follow-up cadences. Help qualify inbound leads from the website and LinkedIn. Build and maintain a CRM view of the pipeline. Take discovery call notes, turn them into structured scoping docs, and draft proposals from templates. Help prep demos and case studies. Light project coordination once an engagement starts.



Across both businesses

Content support. Shape voice memos and rough drafts into Tuesday LinkedIn posts. Direct, no fluff, Hormozi-adjacent. Repurpose into shorter formats. BD list maintenance: 5 new companies per day, trigger event research (funding, leadership changes), draft outreach for review.



You're a fit if

You already use Claude or ChatGPT every day for real work, not just questions. You've touched some of: Make, Zapier, n8n, Airtable, Notion, a CRM, or basic API/MCP setups. You write clean English with personality (we'll test this). You're organized enough to juggle 5 active searches and a consulting pipeline without losing threads. You're comfortable with B2B sales motion (cold outreach, follow-up sequences, qualification) or you've sold something before and want to do more of it. You're curious about both hedge fund hiring and AI tooling. You're available for at least 4 hours of overlap with US Eastern time daily.



Bonus points

Recruiting agency or staffing firm experience. Familiar with Bullhorn, Loxo, Crelate, or similar. B2B SaaS or agency sales support experience. You've written content for someone else's voice. You've built or used AI agents, not just chatted with them. You can read a Python or JavaScript snippet without panicking.



Logistics

Full-time, 40 hours per week, fully remote. Significant overlap with US Eastern hours required. Compensation competitive for your location, tell us what you need. Long-term role with real growth as both businesses scale, you'd be operations lead within 12 months if it clicks.



How to apply

Send a short message (not a generic resume blast) covering:



1. The most useful AI workflow you've built or run for a previous client or yourself. One paragraph.



2. A sample LinkedIn InMail you'd send to a senior C++ engineer at a HFT firm who hasn't responded to two prior messages. 75 words max.



3.  A cold
 you'd send to a Director of Operations at a 200-person logistics company about exploring AI workflow consulting. 100 words max.



Your hourly or monthly rate expectation and earliest start date.

title: Full-Time Content Creator (Nightlife, Events & Comedy) – Virtual Assistant
description:
Type: Full Time | Hours: 38 per week | Salary: $1,200 AUD/month | Paid: Weekly via Wise



?? READ THIS FIRST

If your portfolio is corporate explainers, fitness content, real estate walkthroughs, or talking-head videos — this is not the role for you.

We need someone who lives and breathes nightlife content, comedy-driven ads, and fast-turnaround event promotion. If that's not your lane, save us both the time.



About the Role

We are looking for a full-time Content Creator Virtual Assistant to own the entire content output for a single nightclub venue in Australia.

This is not a "cut clips and add subtitles" role. This is a full content creation role where you own the content engine for one venue.

You will be responsible for producing the complete content pipeline — paid ad creatives, organic social posts, short-form video (Reels, TikTok, Shorts), stories, event hype sequences, post-event recaps, UGC curation, and comedy/personality content that builds the venue's brand between events.

The venue runs on a weekly cycle. Every week has events. Every week needs a content arc — from teaser to hype to event night to recap. You need to understand that rhythm and own it without being told what to post every day.

This is a remote role based in the Philippines. You will not be on-site. Raw footage, photos, and assets will be provided to you by the venue team. However, you are expected to be proactive — you tell the venue what footage you need, what angles to capture, what 
nts to film. You direct the content pipeline. You don't just wait for files to land in your inbox.



This Role Is a Content Engine — Not Just Editing

This is not a general VA role.

This is not a strategy role.

This is not a "wait for briefs and execute" role.

The priority is:



Owning the weekly content cadence for the venue — you know what needs to go out and when

Building multi-day hype sequences before every event (teasers, DJ reveals, countdowns, last-call urgency posts)

Creating scroll-stopping paid ad content (video and static) designed to sell tickets

Producing high-energy organic social posts that drive engagement and build the venue's personality

Editing raw event footage into polished, fast-paced content — Reels, TikToks, Shorts, Stories

Creating post-event recaps, highlight reels, and UGC compilations that keep the venue top of mind

Producing comedy-driven and entertainment-style content that builds the brand between events

Proactively sending shot lists, content briefs, and footage requests to the venue team — you direct what gets captured on the ground

Sourcing and curating user-generated content from patrons (tagged stories, crowd footage, phone videos) and turning it into polished posts and ads



You must understand what makes people stop scrolling, laugh, share, and buy tickets.



Key Responsibilities

Primary Responsibilities (Core Focus)



Owning the weekly content calendar and storytelling arc for the venue

Sending content briefs, shot lists, and footage requests to the venue team before events — you tell them what you need captured

Editing short-form video content for Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts from raw footage provided by the venue

Producing paid ad creatives (video and static) designed to convert and sell tickets

Creating organic social media posts that drive engagement

Building multi-day event hype sequences — teasers, DJ lineup reveals, countdown content, last-call urgency posts

Producing post-event recap content — highlight reels, UGC compilations, crowd reaction clips, best-of edits

Sourcing, curating, and repurposing user-generated content (patron videos, tagged stories, crowd footage)

Creating comedy, personality, and behind-the-scenes content that builds the venue's brand between events

Adding text overlays, subtitles, effects, transitions, and trending audio

Adapting one concept across multiple formats (Feed, Story, Square, Vertical, 9:16)

Delivering content on tight turnarounds — 6 to 24 hours when events demand it



Secondary Responsibilities (Support Tasks)



Organising content files in Google Drive

Tracking deliverables and deadlines in ClickUp

Following creative briefs and campaign deadlines from the marketing team

Preparing content exports correctly for each platform

Scheduling-ready content handoffs



(No paid ads management. No media buying. No marketing strategy.)



Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)

To be considered, you must have:



Experience creating content for nightclubs, DJs, festivals, live events, bars, comedy nights, or entertainment venues

Strong short-form video editing skills — not just trimming clips, but concept-to-finished-piece from raw footage

Experience producing paid ad creatives (video and/or static) that are designed to sell tickets or drive event attendance

Experience creating organic social content that drives real engagement

Understanding of weekly content cadence — you know how to tell a story across a week, building from teaser to hype to recap

Understanding of what makes comedy, entertainment, and nightlife content perform on social

Ability to work fast — this is an events business, not a "we'll get to it next week" environment

The ability to be proactive — you don't wait for someone to tell you what to make. You identify what's needed, request the right footage, and deliver

Evidence of content you've created that has gone viral or achieved significant reach (views, shares, engagement well above the norm for the account). Include specific examples with metrics if possible.

A portfolio that clearly demonstrates nightlife, events, comedy, or entertainment content



If your experience is mainly corporate, ecommerce product ads, fitness, real estate, or minimalist branding — this role is not suitable.



Nice to Have (Not Required)



Experience with ticket sale campaigns or event countdown sequences

Understanding of
/Instagram ad formats and specs

Experience creating UGC-style or native-feeling ad content

Motion graphics or animation skills

Experience working with Australian or Western nightlife brands

Basic graphic design ability (Canva or similar) for event posters, story graphics, and static ad creatives





Content Style We're Looking For



Fast-paced and high-energy

Bold text, punchy captions

Dark themes, neon, club lighting

Comedy timing and entertainment hooks

Native to the platform — not repurposed corporate content

Content that tells a story across the week, not random one-off posts

Content that feels like it belongs on a nightclub's page, not a brand agency's portfolio





What Success Looks Like in This Role



You own the content rhythm for the venue without being micromanaged

You proactively request the footage and assets you need from the venue team

You can turn raw footage into multiple pieces of polished content quickly

You understand the weekly cycle: tease ? hype ? event ? recap ? repeat

You consistently meet tight deadlines

You produce high-quality work without excessive revisions

You create content that gets people to stop scrolling and buy tickets

You've created content before that has achieved viral or outsized reach





Tools You'll Use



CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or similar (video editing)

Adobe Photoshop / Illustrator or Canva (graphics)

Google Drive (file management and asset sharing)

ClickUp (task management)





Working Hours & Compensation



Full-time role (38 hours per week)

Must work during Australian business hours — AWST, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (full overlap required)

Must be responsive and available during agreed working hours

Salary: $1,200 AUD per month, paid weekly via Wise

Long-term position for the right person, with room to grow



This role is not suitable for freelancers juggling multiple clients. We need someone committed, available, and fast.



How to Apply (Important – Read Carefully)

To apply, you must include:



A short introduction about yourself

Links to nightlife, event, comedy, or entertainment content you have created (not just edited)

At least one example of content you've created that went viral or achieved significant reach — include the metrics (views, shares, engagement)

At least one example of a paid ad creative you produced

At least one example of organic social content

An example of how you've proactively directed or briefed content capture (e.g. shot lists, content requests, footage briefs you've sent to a client or team)

The tools you used to create those examples

Your internet speed and equipment setup



Applications without relevant nightlife, event, or entertainment samples will not be considered.

To confirm you've read this job description, include the word "FREQUENCY" in the first line of your application.



Final Note

Do not send a generic portfolio and hope we find the relevant work. Curate your best nightlife, events, comedy, and entertainment pieces and put them front and centre.

If we have to dig through your portfolio to figure out whether you've done this kind of work before — you're already out.

title: Order Manager-Remote (Work-from-Home)-35000PHP- 200,000PHP/month /w Experience-Plus Commission
description:

Travel Visa Pro (
) is a leading US-Passport and Travel Visa Expeditor in the United States of America.



We are seeking Order Manager trainees to join our dynamic National Executive Service Team (NEST).This career path will start with training & data entry duties for a minimum of 30 days. 

     Expert training will be provided to help you advance first to Customer Service representative & then to Order Manager. A custom training plan will be created for every candidate. Travel Visa Pro has a robust training program will provide ample informative training materials. Advancement will come with pay increase as well as commission opportunities. 



    As a Order Manager, you will be responsible for providing exceptional customer support to our clients in the United States of America. You will interact with customers via telephone, online chat, video chat, &
, providing prompt & efficient assistance. Your primary focus will be to guide the client through the visa & passport process. You will be expertly trained on the documents, information & steps needed to guide the client to a successful order completion. 

The general order process steps are:

1.Call the client to "Activate the order" by introducing yourself & going over the steps in the order process & providing the order completion timeline.

2.Verbally instruct the client as to what information & supporting documents you will need from them.

3.Send the client an
 that summarizes the phone conversation & lists the needed information & documentation.

4.Review the supporting documents & information the client provided & follow up with the client on missing documentation.

5.Submit the clients file to a form filler who will complete your clients forms for you.

6.Review the clients application & supporting documents for errors & take action to correct these errors.

7.Make any needed appointments for the client.

8.After the appointment,
 the client their PDF order file. Instruct the client to print the PDF, sign the application & use the included shipping label  to send their file to our V.I.P location for submission to the Embassy or U.S Department of State.

9.Monitor the clients submission progress & inform them when their completed order will arrive at their home.

10.After the order is complete, call the client to thank them for their business, answer any questions & ask them to review us!



If you excel at being proactive, following guidelines, meeting deadlines, de-escalation & resolving issues, upselling, follow-up, verbal/written communication & are committed to providing a positive customer experience, THIS IS THE JOB FOR YOU! 



Responsibilities:

mbers to ensure efficient customer support operations.

• Proficient in using chat &
 as communication channels.

Benefits:

title: Manual QA Tester
description:
Acclaim is a cozy crew of about 15 folks who are all about crafting super optimized, conversion-focused websites using Webflow, WordPress, and AI tools. We're a bunch of passionate people getting it done!





WHO WE ARE:





Since the beginning 2014 we work in a no-office model. Each member of our team works  100% remotely from a convenient place in Poland. In terms of WordPress, we hate predefined themes, that’s why we create bespoke projects based on our framework which has been developed for more than 7 years. 





The most common recipients of our services are Startups, Software Houses, Agencies and other customers from all over the world. Most of our customers are based in the US, UK, Canada, Australia. All of the projects are managed by a Project Manager and tested by a QA Tester. We settle the working time using Toggl, communicate through Slack, and organize tasks in ClickUp. For more details about our business,  
 





In Acclaim, we are searching for a superhero handyman who can provide us with full-stack WordPress skills. We are looking for someone who feels comfortable with front-end development and back-end development. Do you think you could be the right fit? 



YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:



- Perform manual tests (functional tests, usability tests, visual tests, cross-browsers tests) of website, eCommerce stores, and web applications (Responsive Web Design, Pixel perfect coding, Animations)

- Analyze business and technical documentation (in English) as well as graphic designs or prototypes

- Identification of issues and bugs, and the ability to properly report them 

- Constantly looking for new ways of testing our projects





OUR EXPECTATIONS:



- Attention to detail and quality of work are MUST HAVE

-  Great communication skills including good command of English – at least B2

-  At least 2 years of commercial experience in Quality Assurance testing

-  Knowledge of technical language that allows for efficient communication with the team and the client

-  Logical thinking and perceptiveness skills

-  Focus on finding solutions and initiative in action

-  Experience in working remotely

-  Experience with CMS (mostly WordPress -- including WooCommerce, Hubspot, Shopify)





NICE TO HAVE:



-   Ability to work under time pressure

-  Basic knowledge of Front-End (HTML/CSS/JS), and Back-End (PHP) languages

-  Experience in writing automatic tests 

-  Experience as a Graphic/Product Designer

-  Knowledge of bug tracking and project management tools (e.g. ClickUp, Asana, JIRA)





LEARN MORE ABOUT US:





For more information about Acclaim & our amazing projects, check out our LinkedIn  
 



If you want to get to know us, even further check out what our customers have to say about us 
 



OUR KEY VALUES:





- Focus on the highest quality of coding -  web optimization is our beacon!

- Prioritising top-level customer service

- Delivering the projects on time





OUR KEY SERVICES:



- UI/UX Web Design

- Webflow Development

- AI Development

- Wordpress Support & Maintenance

- Figma/XD to WordPress Conversion

- WordPress Plugin Development

- SEO

title: Controller
description:
Quantum Accounting Associates is a complete outsourced accounting firm providing bookkeeping, controller, and fractional CFO services to growth-minded businesses. We partner with multiple clients across industries to provide financial clarity, improve processes, and support better decision-making.

We are looking for a Controller who is highly organized, analytical, client-focused, and experienced in managing accounting operations for multiple clients in a fast-paced environment.

Position Overview

The Controller will oversee accounting functions for a portfolio of clients, manage financial reporting, support month-end close, improve processes, and deliver financial analysis that helps clients understand their numbers and make informed decisions.

Responsibilities

• Manage month-end and year-end close for multiple client accounts

• Review reconciliations, journal entries, accruals, and financial statements

• Ensure accuracy of general ledger activity and financial reporting

• Trail Balance review/deep dive

• Support budgeting, forecasting, and cash flow reporting

• Analyze financial data, trends, and variances

• Prepare KPI dashboards and management reports

• Identify process improvement opportunities and implement best practices

• Work closely with clients, internal tea
mbers, and leadership

• Maintain strong client communication and manage deadlines across multiple engagements

• Assist with system optimization, documentation, and special projects

Qualifications

• MUST work EST

• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

• 5+ years of progressive accounting experience

• 2+ years in a Controller, Assistant Controller, or senior accounting role preferred

• Strong understanding of GAAP and financial reporting

• Experience managing multiple clients or entities preferred

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

• Advanced Excel skills required

• Experience with QuickBooks Online and other cloud-based systems preferred

• Strong communication and client service skills

Why Join Us

At Quantum Accounting Associates, we help clients turn financial data into clarity and 
ntum. This is a great opportunity for a skilled accounting professional who enjoys working across multiple businesses, solving problems, and providing meaningful insight.

title: Sales & VIP Customer Support Specialist  (Premium Travel & Events Company)
description:
We're Event Escapes, a premium event-driven travel platform based in Melbourne, Australia. We package hotel stays, event tickets, and curated experiences for major global sporting events like F1, MotoGP, FIFA World Cup, Premier League, NBA, Tennis Grand Slams and NFL. We recently launched and are growing fast. We're looking for a motivated, detail-oriented tea
mber to join us part-time and grow with the business.



What You'll Be Doing:



Customer Support & VIP Concierge:

You'll provide premium, white-glove phone support for our VIP clients. These are high-value bookings like F1 weekend packages and World Cup hospitality. For standard customers, you'll handle
 support ensuring fast and professional responses. You'll also monitor and action support tickets generated by our AI receptionist system, assist with booking enquiries, modifications and post-booking follow-ups, and deliver a luxury concierge-level experience that matches our brand.



Sales & Lead Generation:

You'll follow up on inbound leads generated through social media campaigns and paid ads, engaging warm leads via
, WhatsApp and other channels to convert enquiries into bookings. As we scale you'll support outbound initiatives too, identifying potential clients, partnerships and group booking opportunities. You'll track and report on your lead pipeline and conversion.



What We're Looking For:

Excellent spoken and written English is essential as you'll be speaking with Australian, Singaporean and international clients. You need a confident and professional phone manner because this is a premium brand. We want someone who is sales-minded with a customer-first attitude and comfortable using modern tools.



You must be highly reliable and self-motivated since you'll be working remotely with a small, fast-moving team. Familiarity with the travel, events or hospitality industry is a strong plus. You need to be available to work some weekends as events happen on weekends and this is non-negotiable. You should also be willing to be on-call occasionally for VIP client support during live events.



Requirements

You must have your own laptop with minimum specs to handle a modern browser, video calls and multi-tab workflow. Reliable high-speed internet at all times with no exceptions. A quiet environment for phone calls with VIP clients. Weekend availability on a rotating basis.



Hours & Compensation

Starting at 10 to 12 hours per week with gradual increase as the business grows. Salary is PHP 40,000 to 44,000 per month, pro-rated based on actual hours worked. Additional hours beyond the agreed schedule are compensated pro rata. Schedule is flexible but must include some weekend coverage. This is a long-term role with real growth potential and we want someone who wants to build something with us.



Why Join Event Escapes?

You're getting in on the ground floor of a launched startup. Not a concept, we're live and selling. You'll work with premium global events like F1, World Cup, NBA and Grand Slams. It's a small team where your work directly impacts growth. We use a modern tech stack with AI tools that make your job easier, not harder. There's a clear path to full-time as we scale into Singapore, Malaysia and beyond.



How to Apply



Please include:



A short introduction

Your experience in customer support and/or sales

A brief example of how you would handle an unhappy customer

Your weekly availability.
